name: S I Philadelphia
description_short: Federal credit union in Philadelphia serving 75 members with $212K in assets, offering checking, savings, and basic financial services since 1957.
description_long:
S I Philadelphia Federal Credit Union is a small, member-owned federal credit union established in 1957, now operating for 69 years. It is headquartered at 636 N 43rd St in Philadelphia, PA 19104, with a single branch location. The credit union is chartered as a federal institution (Charter #11783) and maintains an associational-religious membership type, though not designated as low-income.

The credit union offers basic banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and loan products. Based on their most recent financial statement (as of March 1, 2025), they hold approximately $2,029 in total loans outstanding, $135,182 in member shares and deposits, and maintain $212,106 in total assets. The organization generated $1,248 in net income and maintains equity capital of $76,924.

What distinguishes S I Philadelphia is its hyper-local focus and long operational history in a specific Philadelphia community. With only 75 members and minimal assets compared to larger credit unions, this institution operates as a tight-knit financial cooperative rather than a commercial banking alternative. Leadership includes CEO/Manager Marlene E Long and Chairperson Eva Farland. The credit union is not a member of the Federal Home Loan Bank system and carries standard NCUSIF insurance protections.

This credit union is best suited for existing members within its specific associational-religious network seeking basic savings and checking services. The extremely small asset base ($212K), minimal loan portfolio ($2K), and 75-member restriction mean this is not a viable option for new members seeking competitive rates or comprehensive financial services. Prospective members should verify eligibility within the stated associational membership requirement. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Federal credit union with NCUSIF insurance protection for member deposits
69-year operating history demonstrates institutional stability and community presence
Member-owned cooperative structure with no external shareholders
Offers ATM/debit card access for convenient cash and payment options
Local Philadelphia headquarters serving the West Philadelphia community since 1957
Maintains both checking and savings account options for members
cons: Extremely limited assets ($212K) restrict lending capacity and financial product breadth
Only 75 members means highly restrictive associational-religious membership eligibility
Single branch location with no online banking presence or alternative access channels
Minimal loan portfolio ($2K outstanding) indicates very limited credit availability
No website listed, making it difficult for prospective members to research services independently
best_for: Existing members within the credit union's associational-religious community seeking basic savings
Individuals wanting to support a hyper-local, community-rooted financial institution
Members requiring simple checking and ATM/debit card services only
diagnosis: S I Philadelphia Federal Credit Union is exclusively for pre-existing members within its associational-religious network who need basic checking, savings, and debit card services. The critical limitation is membership eligibility—this is not open to the general public and membership verification through the specific association is required before any account opening is possible.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
